mission.title: caddy: http/3 support in reverse_proxy module
mission.description: http/3 support in reverse_proxy module. This issue affects 10+ users.

steps.title: Identify caddy feature symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your caddy deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=caddy
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=caddy -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: http/3 support in reverse_proxy module

steps.title: Check current resource configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant caddy resources:
```bash
kubectl get resource -A
kubectl describe resource <name> -n <namespace>
```
Hi Guys,

I was tweaking with reverse_proxy settings a lot and was unable to proxy http/3 traffic - does RP supports H3?

Cheers,
Daniel

steps.title: Apply the fix for http/3 support in reverse_proxy module
Confirm http/3 support in reverse_proxy module is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=caddy -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: Problem with caddy and h3:
https://github.com/caddyserver/caddy/issues/5086#issuecomment-2094529691

Problem with DoH proxy:
https://github.com/m13253/dns-over-https?tab=readme-ov-file#known-issues

So unless anyone can suggest a way to simultaneously have the two of them (https server and DoH proxy) listen on 443, all I can do is wait for them to get fixed/implemented.. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.
codeSnippets: caddy on vps (Los Angeles) <== wireguard ==> caddy on my home (indonesia)

prerequisites.description: A working caddy installation or development environment.
